title: Image Names and Tags
description:
## Decoding an Image Reference

Consider the image reference:

```
registry.example.com:5000/team/api:2.3.1
```

Which breakdown is correct?

options.text: It is invalid — image references cannot contain a port

explanation: A fully-qualified reference is `[registry[:port]/]repository[:tag]`. Here the registry host and port are `registry.example.com:5000`, the repository is `team/api`, and the tag is `2.3.1`. If you omit the registry, Docker defaults to Docker Hub; if you omit the tag, it defaults to `latest`. Note that `latest` is just a conventional tag — it is **not** automatically the newest image.

hints.title: Add a tag to an existing image
hints.body: docker tag creates an additional reference (name/tag) pointing at the same image ID.
